title: hieracium triangulare subsp. subtriangulare
intro: Hieracium triangulare subsp. subtriangulare, also known as Hieracium, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), typically found in Europe and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Hieracium lie in its leaves, flowers, and roots, which have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and improving skin conditions, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Hieracium include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
